title: Source re-verification and term-refresh cadence (terminology evolves)
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual's allow-listed sources and oncology/clinical-trial terminology evolve over time. Definitions that are accurate today may become outdated or a source license may change, requiring affected entries to be withdrawn. A documented maintenance cadence ensures the glossary remains accurate and correctly licensed after delivery, using stored snapshots and the automated watcher (watcher-001) to detect drift.
objective: Document a maintenance cadence for re-verifying allow-list sources (detect upstream or license changes via stored snapshots) and refreshing term entries as oncology terminology evolves, including the withdraw, correct, re-review, re-publish procedure for post-delivery inaccuracies and a license-change withdrawal procedure.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/maintenance-cadence.md is created defining: frequency of allow-list source re-verification (at minimum annually, or triggered by watcher-001 hash-drift alert), procedure for detecting and responding to license changes (withdraw affected entries, record reason, update allow-list), and the term-refresh process when oncology terminology evolves

Document defines the withdraw, correct, re-review, re-publish procedure for post-delivery inaccuracies: (1) withdraw the affected entry from publication, (2) correct the definition, (3) re-review through the same clinical + advocate gate and translation gate if applicable, (4) re-publish with updated provenance

License-change withdrawal procedure is documented: if a source's license changes to forbid reuse, affected entries are immediately flagged, removed from distribution, and the impact assessed using provenance records
Cadence references the source-change watcher (watcher-001) and the allow-list schema (sources-001) for detecting hash drift
resources: PLAN.md — Data, licensing and compliance section
output: docs/maintenance-cadence.md with source re-verification schedule, term-refresh process, and post-delivery correction and license-change withdrawal procedures
